prompt: Write the opening paragraph of a children's book about a shy octopus who wants to make friends.
response: Deep beneath the shimmering waves, where sunlight danced in golden ribbons and coral castles bloomed in every color of the rainbow, lived a little octopus named Ollie. Ollie had eight wiggly arms, eyes as big and soft as moonstones, and a heart full of wishes—but he was terribly, wonderfully shy. Every day, he watched from behind a swaying curtain of kelp as seahorses played tag, clownfish giggled in anemone hideouts, and dolphins somersaulted through the surf. He longed to join them, to share his favorite seashell jokes or show off his bubble-blowing tricks… but whenever he tried to swim closer, his arms would tangle with nerves, and he’d dart back into the quiet safety of his cozy cave. Still, deep down, Ollie knew one thing for sure: he really, truly wanted a friend.
